evidence: Confirmed contract dishonesty: `updateToolSettingsSupabase` accepts `_signal?: AbortSignal` but drops it (`src/shared/hooks/useToolSettings.ts:150-163`), while callers create/pass abort signals expecting cancellation semantics (`src/shared/hooks/useToolSettings.ts:423-441`).

Confirmed cross-module API drift in lightbox contracts: `MediaGalleryLightboxActionContract.onDelete` is async (`Promise<void>`) and `MediaGalleryLightboxIntegrationContract.onCreateShot` is narrowed to `Promise<void>` (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx:32-44,77-83`), while canonical `MediaLightboxProps` uses `onDelete?: (id)=>void` and richer `onCreateShot` return payload (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx:31,44-45`).

Confirmed behavior gap from drift: image lightbox executes delete as fire-and-forget (`actions?.onDelete?.(media.id)`) with no await/error path (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:687-689`), even when upstream caller contract is async.

Refuted mechanical signal (`useGenerationMutations` async-without-await): async helpers and mutation fns contain awaited DB/scope calls (`src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:58-345`), so flagged smell does not currently indicate an API-coherence defect.

Refuted mechanical signal (`createSupabaseClient` async-without-await): `dataFetch` is intentionally promise-returning adapter for Supabase fetch replacement and does not require internal await (`src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/createSupabaseClient.ts:52-61`).

Refuted mechanical signal (`ProjectSelectorPopover.handleKeyDown` signature variance): this is component-local React keyboard handler usage, not a shared exported API surface (`src/shared/components/GlobalHeader/ProjectSelectorPopover.tsx:50-62`).

Refuted mechanical signal (`ReferenceThumbnail.handleTouchEnd` signature variance): local React touch handler, not a reusable boundary contract (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/components/reference/ReferenceThumbnail.tsx:66-75`).

Refuted mechanical signal (`generateUniqueFilename` mismatch): file exposes `generateUniqueLoraFilename`, while generic filename API is distinct in storage util (`src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/utils/validation-utils.ts:49`, `src/shared/lib/storagePaths.ts:9`).

Refuted mechanical signal (`MediaGalleryLightbox` void-suppressed symbol): `void cacheVersion` is used to tie memo recomputation to cache subscription updates; awkward but functionally intentional (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx:192-207,236`).

Refuted mechanical signal (`StrokeOverlay` size/hooks): file is large and complex but the visible issues are decomposition/readability, not primary API-shape incoherence in this batch dimension (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx`).

findings.summary: Delete confirmation contract narrows base dialog API and hides async/loading behavior.
findings.suggestion: Promote a single confirm contract used by both base and delete dialogs: include async `onConfirm` and explicit pending state (`isConfirming`), then thread it through `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` and all call sites so delete UX and API shape are consistent.
evidence: `DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` defines `onConfirm: () => void` only.
`ConfirmDialog` supports `onConfirm: () => void | Promise<void>` plus `isLoading`.
`DeleteGenerationConfirmDialog` re-exports the narrow contract and cannot pass loading state.

Consumers therefore split behavior inconsistently (`isPending` boolean in travel view vs `deletingId` in gallery/join-clips), because dialog-level contract cannot represent in-flight confirm semantics.

findings.summary: Join-clips pending action API documents discriminated actions but exports only opaque clip payloads.
findings.suggestion: Make the contract honest in one direction: either return a true discriminated union with explicit `type`, or update docs/types to explicitly state action placement is resolved only by `applyPendingClipActions` and keep both functions as a single cohesive API surface.
evidence: Docstring says each action is `{ type: 'fill' | 'append', clip }` (`clipInitService.ts:196-203`).
Actual exported type is `PendingClipAction { clip: VideoClip }` without `type` (`204-206`).
Placement decision is deferred to `applyPendingClipActions` (`315-349`), so API callers cannot reason about intent from the returned contract.

findings.summary: Boolean task-param API coerces malformed numeric inputs to false, hiding contract violations.
findings.suggestion: Tighten contract parsing: accept only `0|1` for numeric boolean encodings, return `null` for other numbers, and let `extractAddInPositionParam` surface/telemetry invalid payloads before defaulting.
evidence: `extractTaskParamBoolean` treats any number as boolean by `value === 1`; non-1 numbers become `false` (`taskParamContract.ts:123-131`).
`extractAddInPositionParam` then defaults null to false (`157-159`), so malformed values and explicit false collapse to the same result.
Tests only cover `0` and `1` encodings (`taskParamContract.test.ts:43-48`), leaving undefined behavior for malformed numeric values.

findings.summary: A shared deletion API used across multiple features lacks direct behavior tests.
findings.suggestion: Add focused hook tests covering: `requestDelete` opens dialog, `onOpenChange(false)` cancels, `confirmDelete` clears pending state and forwards mutation args, and pending/deleting signals are consistent across repeated calls.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` is consumed in gallery, join-clips, travel-between-images, image-generation, and character-animate paths.
Existing tests typically mock the hook output instead of validating its own contract transitions (`open` derivation, cancel path, confirm path).
Given broad reuse, regressions in this hook would fan out across multiple tool surfaces without an owning contract test.

findings.summary: Settings update API advertises cancellation but silently ignores the passed AbortSignal.
findings.suggestion: Make the contract honest in one direction: either remove the signal parameter and abort-controller plumbing from callers, or add cancellation support end-to-end (include signal in queued write payload and enforce abort checks before flush/write execution).
evidence: `updateToolSettingsSupabase(params, _signal, mode)` accepts a signal parameter but never threads it anywhere (`src/shared/hooks/useToolSettings.ts:150-163`).

`useToolSettings.update` creates AbortControllers and passes `controller.signal` into the mutation path (`src/shared/hooks/useToolSettings.ts:423-441`), implying callers expect cancellation to be meaningful.
Queue/write layer (`QueuedWrite`, `enqueueSettingsWrite`) has no signal field and cannot cancel in-flight/pending writes (`src/shared/lib/settingsWriteQueue.ts:17-22,133-174`).

findings.summary: Lightbox callback contracts are redefined across layers with conflicting async and return-shape semantics.
findings.suggestion: Define a single exported callback contract source for lightbox integrations (e.g., shared adapter contract types), import it from both MediaGallery and Timeline, and eliminate local redefinitions that narrow or alter async/return semantics.
evidence: Feature-layer contract defines `onDelete?: (id) => Promise<void>` and narrows `onCreateShot` to `Promise<void>` (`MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx:33,78`).
Canonical lightbox API defines `onDelete?: (id) => void` and `onCreateShot?: Promise<{shotId?: string; shotName?: string} | void>` (`MediaLightbox.tsx:31,44`).
Timeline’s sibling integration still uses the richer `onCreateShot` payload (`Timeline.tsx:71`), showing divergence between callers to the same adapter target.
Adapter simply spreads partial groups into props (`MediaLightboxInputAdapter.tsx:102-112`), so there is no normalization layer to reconcile these mismatches.

findings.summary: Async delete handlers passed into lightbox are invoked as sync fire-and-forget with no failure channel.
findings.suggestion: Standardize `onDelete` as async in canonical lightbox props and await it where invoked (with explicit loading/error handling), or strictly enforce sync behavior at all call sites and remove async typed variants.
evidence: Media gallery side provides delete callback as async (`onDelete?: (id) => Promise<void>`) (`MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx:33`).
Core lightbox prop types erase async intent (`onDelete?: (id) => void`) (`MediaLightbox.tsx:31`).
Image lightbox executes delete without await/error handling (`actions?.onDelete?.(media.id)`) (`ImageLightbox.tsx:687-689`), so promise rejections cannot be surfaced through this boundary.
